Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Building a Blog with Elixir and Phoenix 41 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. OpenTelemetry profiles enters public alpha 90 upvotes, 10 comments
  3. Why so many control rooms were seafoam green (2025) 284 upvotes, 44 comments
  4. Personal Encyclopedias 732 upvotes, 147 comments
  5. We Haven't Seen the Worst of What Gambling and Prediction Markets Will Do 24 upvotes, 1 comments
  6. Obsolete Sounds 188 upvotes, 35 comments
  7. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 814 upvotes, 283 comments
  8. Doom entirely from DNS records 92 upvotes, 24 comments
  9. From zero to a RAG system: successes and failures 237 upvotes, 75 comments
  10. Interoperability Can Save the Open Web (2023) 149 upvotes, 44 comments
  11. Stripe Projects: Provision and manage services from the CLI 65 upvotes, 16 comments
  12. My home network observes bedtime with OpenBSD and pf 81 upvotes, 26 comments
  13. Colibri – chat platform built on the AT Protocol for communities big and small 72 upvotes, 25 comments
  14. Taming LLMs: Using Executable Oracles to Prevent Bad Code 11 upvotes, 1 comments
  15. Shell Tricks That Make Life Easier (and Save Your Sanity) 433 upvotes, 211 comments
  16. My minute-by-minute response to the LiteLLM malware attack 192 upvotes, 88 comments
  17. Moving from GitHub to Codeberg, for lazy people 418 upvotes, 212 comments
  18. HyperAgents: Self-referential self-improving agents 57 upvotes, 21 comments
  19. End of "Chat Control": EU parliament stops mass surveillance 444 upvotes, 231 comments
  20. John Bradley, author of xv, has passed away 60 upvotes, 25 comments
  21. Show HN: Turbolite – a SQLite VFS serving sub-250ms cold JOIN queries from S3 20 upvotes, 6 comments
  22. Swift 6.3 282 upvotes, 186 comments
  23. Light on Glass: Why do you start making a game engine? 32 upvotes, 15 comments
  24. Fermented foods shaped human biology 33 upvotes, 21 comments
  25. CERN to host Europe's flagship open access publishing platform 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  26. Show HN: Orloj – agent infrastructure as code (YAML and GitOps) 10 upvotes, 6 comments
  27. Show HN: Layerleak – Like Trufflehog, but for Docker Hub 4 upvotes, 1 comments
  28. How much precision can you squeeze out of a table? 4 upvotes, 1 comments
  29. Olympic Committee bars transgender athletes from women’s events 103 upvotes, 244 comments
  30. Ask HN: Running legacy IE/ActiveX clients without local admin rights? 3 upvotes, 4 comments

Last generated 4:00am, 27 March 2026. Source here.
